mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator designed to simplify the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It provides custom resource definitions for managing Ray clusters, jobs, and services, making it ideal for machine learning workloads. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential complexity in managing Ray applications.

steps.title: Add the KubeRay Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeRay repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay
steps.description:
This step installs KubeRay using Helm, specifying the version and namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kuberay-system

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay-system --version 1.5.1

```

> Creates a namespace for KubeRay resources.
> Installs KubeRay in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay installation
steps.description:
This step checks if KubeRay components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay-system

```

> Lists all pods in the KubeRay namespace to verify they are running.

steps.title: Create a RayCluster
steps.description:
This step creates a RayCluster custom resource to manage Ray workloads.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kuberay.io/v1alpha1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: example-raycluster
  namespace: kuberay-system
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Creates a RayCluster with 1 head and 2 worker nodes.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kuberay.io/v1alpha1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: example-raycluster
  namespace: kuberay-system
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all RayClusters and their data.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay-system

kubectl delete namespace kuberay-system

```

> Uninstalls KubeRay from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the KubeRay namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up existing resources, upgrading KubeRay, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay-system -o yaml > raycluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay-system --version 1.5.1

kubectl get pods -n kuberay-system

```

> Backs up existing RayCluster configurations.
> Upgrades KubeRay to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay-system -o yaml > raycluster-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating RayCluster: Failed to create head pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Kubernetes cluster does not have sufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory resources available for the RayCluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RayCluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RayCluster pods are not starting due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the RayCluster pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kuberay-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Ray cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate network issues or misconfigured service endpoints.

**Fix:**
Verify the service endpoints and network policies in your Kubernetes cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: no matches for kind 'RayCluster' in version 'kuberay.io/v1alpha1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the KubeRay CRDs are not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall KubeRay using the Helm chart to ensure CRDs are set up properly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Ray features and improved stability of RayCluster management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RayCluster status as 'Running' when you execute 'kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay-system'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
